question:
Will IT's market close price on each resolution date listed below be higher than its market close price on 2026-03-01?

Stock splits and reverse splits will be accounted for in resolving this question. Forecasts on questions about companies that have been delisted (through mergers or bankruptcy) will resolve to their final close price.
resolution_criteria:
Resolves to the market close price at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/IT for the resolution date. If the resolution date coincides with a day the market is closed (weekend, holiday, etc.) the previous market close price is used.

The exact value on 2026-03-01 is unknown, but the most recent known value (as of 2026-02-19) was 161.250. (The latest market close price of IT.)
background: Gartner, Inc. provides business and technology insights for decisions and performance on an organization's mission-critical priorities in the United States, Canada, Europe, the Middle East, Africa, and internationally. It operates through three segments: Insights, Conferences, and Consulting. The Research segment delivers insights through subscription services, such as access to published content, data and benchmarks, and direct access to a network of business and technology experts. The Conferences segment offers executives and teams in an organization the opportunity to learn, share, and network through its Symposium/Xpo series and peer-driven sessions, as well as conferences focused on specific business roles and topics. The Consulting segment provides technology-driven strategic initiatives, including custom analysis and on-the-ground support to senior executives. This segment also offers actionable solutions for IT-related priorities, including IT cost optimization, digital transformation, and IT sourcing optimization. Gartner, Inc. was founded in 1979 and is headquartered in Stamford, Connecticut.
